purpose: Live dashboard showing each student's lab submission status per day. Trainer uses it during class to see who is done, who is stuck, and who earned the gold crown.

how_it_works.step_1: dashboard/index.html loads in the browser and calls /api/submissions on page load and on every Refresh click.
how_it_works.step_2: api/submissions.py hits the GitHub API — fetches all forks of Anilmidna/sigma-genai-de, then for each fork checks whether the expected output files exist.
how_it_works.step_3: Status is computed per student per day: master (crown), complete (tick), partial (tilde), missing (red-x).
how_it_works.step_4: For manual days (e.g. presentation days with no file submissions), api/manual_overrides.json is read instead. Trainer edits that file and pushes — Vercel redeploys in ~60 seconds.
how_it_works.note: Vercel runs submissions.py as a serverless function on every GET /api/submissions. There is no database — all state comes from GitHub API + manual_overrides.json.

files.vercel.json: Routing config. Maps /api/submissions → submissions.py, /api/debug → debug.py, everything else → dashboard/index.html. Edit here to add new API routes.
files.api/submissions.py: Core logic. Two entry points: build_response() (called by app.py locally) and handler class (called by Vercel serverless). Edit EXPECTED_FILES to add output files for a new day. Edit MASTER_TIER to define crown vs tick threshold per day.
files.api/manual_overrides.json: Trainer-edited file for days with no GitHub submissions (e.g. presentations). Keys: _manual_days (list of day numbers to bypass GitHub), _teams (name→member mapping), _day_labels (display label per day), then one key per day number with student real_name→grade pairs. Valid grades: master, complete, partial, missing.
files.api/students.csv: Maps github_username → real_name. Add a row when a new student forks the repo. Two columns: github_username, real_name.
files.api/debug.py: Serverless endpoint at /api/debug. Returns JSON: whether GITHUB_TOKEN is set, its length, TRAINER_REPO value, and students.csv row count. Use this first when something looks wrong.
files.api/app.py: Flask wrapper for local development only. Not used by Vercel. Run: python api/app.py to test locally.
files.api/requirements.txt: Python dependencies for local Flask server (flask, flask-cors). Vercel only needs the stdlib + boto3 which submissions.py imports conditionally.
files.dashboard/index.html: Single-file frontend. Fetches /api/submissions, renders an HTML table. Status icons defined in STATUS_ICON dict (~line 415). Tooltip per cell built by buildTooltip(). Edit CSS variables at top of <style> to retheme. No build step needed — it is plain HTML/JS.
files.set_grade.py: CLI helper for manual grading. Run: python set_grade.py --team team1 master OR python set_grade.py 'Harshit' complete. Edits manual_overrides.json then does git add+commit+push automatically. Vercel redeploys from the push.

environment_variables.GITHUB_TOKEN: Personal access token with repo scope. Set in Vercel dashboard → Project → Settings → Environment Variables. Without this, /api/submissions returns an error JSON.
environment_variables.TRAINER_REPO: Format: owner/repo. Default: Anilmidna/sigma-genai-de. The repo whose forks are scanned. Change this if the trainer repo is renamed or transferred.

adding_a_new_day.step_1: Add an entry to EXPECTED_FILES in api/submissions.py. Key = day number (int). Value = dict of {relative_output_path: display_label}. Paths are relative to dayN/lab/ inside each fork.
adding_a_new_day.step_2: Optionally add to MASTER_TIER if this day has a crown tier (core files for complete, extra files for master).
adding_a_new_day.step_3: Push. Vercel redeploys. The new day column appears in the dashboard automatically.

manual_grading_workflow.use_case: Presentation days, hackathon days, or any day where students do not push files.
manual_grading_workflow.step_1: Add the day number to _manual_days array in api/manual_overrides.json.
manual_grading_workflow.step_3: Add a top-level key for the day number with all student real_names set to 'missing'.
manual_grading_workflow.step_4: During class, run: python set_grade.py --team team1 master (or grade one student at a time).
manual_grading_workflow.step_5: Each run pushes to GitHub. Vercel redeploys in ~60s. Click Refresh on dashboard.
manual_grading_workflow.tip: Run python set_grade.py --status to see current grades without pushing anything.

common_fixes.dashboard_shows_no_data: Check /api/debug first. If GITHUB_TOKEN_set is false, set the env var in Vercel dashboard and redeploy.
common_fixes.student_not_appearing: Add their github_username and real_name to api/students.csv and push.
common_fixes.wrong_name_shown: Fix the real_name in api/students.csv. Names in manual_overrides.json must match real_name exactly (case-insensitive match is applied but exact spelling preferred).
common_fixes.day_column_missing: The day number is not in EXPECTED_FILES or _manual_days. Add it and push.
common_fixes.vercel_deploy_failing: Check Vercel dashboard → Deployments → latest build log. Most common cause: syntax error in submissions.py or malformed manual_overrides.json.
common_fixes.github_api_rate_limit: The token is unauthenticated or expired. Replace GITHUB_TOKEN in Vercel env vars. Authenticated requests get 5000 calls/hour — more than enough for 35 students.
